title: Systems and devices for maintaining an electrical connection
abstract: An electrical connector includes a locking plug, a lock actuator, a strain relief, and an exterior mating surface. The locking plug includes a locking mechanism and the lock actuator is coupled to the locking mechanism. The strain relief is coupled to the locking plug and the exterior mating surface is coupled to the locking plug and axially moveable relative to the locking plug to move the lock actuator.
claims:
Claims 1. An electrical connector, the electrical connector comprising: a locking plug with a locking mechanism; a lock actuator coupled to the locking mechanism; a strain relief coupled to the locking plug; and an exterior mating surface coupled to the locking plug and axially moveable relative to the locking plug to move the lock actuator. 2. The electrical connector of claim 1, wherein the exterior mating surface is configured to actuate the lock actuator though an axial force applied to the exterior mating surface. 3. The electrical connector of claim 1, wherein the strain relief is configured to actuate the lock actuator though an axial force applied to the strain relief by the exterior mating surface. 4. The electrical connector of any preceding claim, wherein an axial force to actuate the lock actuator is greater than 0.20 Newtons. 5. The electrical connector of any preceding claim, wherein an axial force to actuate the lock actuator is less than 2.00 Newtons. 6. The electrical connector of any preceding claim, wherein the strain relief includes a rigid material and a soft material. 7. The electrical connector of claim 6, wherein the soft material has a Young' s modulus of less than 1 GPa. 8. The electrical connector of claim 6, wherein the rigid material has a Young's modulus of greater than 1 GPa. 9. The electrical connector of any preceding claim, wherein the exterior mating surface is positioned circumferentially about the strain relief, and the strain relief is coaxial with a cable in electrical communication with the locking plug. 10. The electrical connector of any preceding claim, further comprising a biasing element configured to bias the exterior mating surface axially toward the locking plug. 11. The electrical connector of any preceding claim, wherein the exterior mating surface has a shoulder that extends laterally from a longitudinal axis of the exterior mating surface. 

 The electrical connector of claim 1 1, wherein the shoulder extends laterally past the perimeter of the locking plug by between 1 and 2.0 times the width of the locking plug. A system, the system comprising: an electronic device having an appliance inlet connector, the appliance inlet connector having an appliance inlet connector surface; and the electrical connector of any preceding claim, wherein electrical connector is configured to mate with the appliance inlet connector, the exterior mating surface having a proximal-facing surface abutting the appliance inlet connector surface in a plugged configuration. The system of claim 13, wherein the electrical connector is an IEC C13 plug, and the appliance inlet connector is an IEC C14 socket. The system of claim 13, wherein a distal-facing surface of the exterior mating surface is shaped to match a profile of an exterior surface of the electronic device.
